HR7.reason: Prohibits taxpayer-funded abortions - bans use of federal funds for abortions and prohibits tax credits or subsidies for health plans that cover abortion, restricting abortion access

HR36.reason: Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act - bans abortions after 20 weeks nationwide based on claim that fetuses can feel pain at that stage, criminalizing the procedure with penalties for providers

HR3504.reason: Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (114th) - requires medical care for infants born after attempted abortions and imposes criminal penalties on providers, designed to restrict abortion access and stigmatize the procedure

HR3134.reason: Defund Planned Parenthood Act of 2015 - imposes a one-year moratorium on federal funding to Planned Parenthood, the largest abortion provider in the US, restricting access to abortion and reproductive health services

HR3762.reason: Budget reconciliation bill (114th) that defunded Planned Parenthood and repealed key ACA provisions including Medicaid expansion - vetoed by President Obama

HR3495.reason: Women's Public Health and Safety Act (114th) - redirected Planned Parenthood funding to other community health centers, effectively defunding Planned Parenthood

S6.reason: Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (119th) - requires medical care for infants born alive after abortion attempts and imposes criminal penalties, an anti-abortion bill that restricts provider practices

HR8297.reason: Ensuring Access to Abortion Act of 2022 - passed after Dobbs decision, prohibits states from restricting individuals from traveling to other states to obtain abortions

HR8296.reason: Women's Health Protection Act of 2022 - codifies right to abortion nationwide by prohibiting states from imposing medically unnecessary restrictions on abortion access

S4132.reason: Women's Health Protection Act (Senate, 117th) - cloture vote to codify abortion rights, protecting a person's ability to determine whether to continue or end a pregnancy

HR3755.reason: Women's Health Protection Act (Senate cloture, 117th) - cloture vote on bill to protect abortion access and prevent states from imposing medically unnecessary restrictions

S4554.reason: Post-Dobbs bill expressing support for protecting access to reproductive health care after the Dobbs decision overturned Roe v. Wade

HR8373.reason: Right to Contraception Act - protects access to contraception, which is related to but not directly about abortion; however, many anti-abortion advocates oppose it as part of reproductive rights framework

S4381.reason: Right to Contraception Act (Senate) - cloture vote to protect access to contraceptives, part of broader reproductive rights after Dobbs

HJRES43.reason: CRA disapproval of Obama-era Title X rule requiring family planning grantees to provide abortion referrals - voting Yea blocks the requirement, allowing grantees to refuse abortion referrals

SJRES41.reason: CRA disapproval of Biden HHS rule 'Ensuring Access to Equitable, Affordable, Client-Centered, Quality Family Planning Services' which restored Title X requirements to provide abortion referrals - voting Yea blocks abortion referral requirements

SJRES10.reason: CRA disapproval of VA rule on 'Reproductive Health Services' which allowed VA to provide abortion services and counseling to veterans - voting Yea blocks VA from providing these services

HCONRES3.reason: Resolution condemning attacks on pro-life facilities, groups, and churches - while not restricting abortion directly, signals opposition to abortion rights movement

S4445.reason: Bill to protect and expand access to fertility treatment including IVF - related to reproductive rights broadly, cloture vote in 118th Congress

HR6918.reason: Supporting Pregnant and Parenting Women and Families Act (118th) - Republican alternative to abortion access that provides support for pregnant women but does not protect abortion rights, framed as pro-life legislation

HR6945.reason: Supporting Pregnant and Parenting Women and Families Act (119th) - Republican pro-life alternative providing support for pregnant women while not protecting abortion access
